title: leucanthemum montanum subsp. atratum
intro: Leucanthemum montanum subsp. atratum, also known as the Mountain Everlasting, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and supporting respiratory health. Leucanthemum montanum subsp. atratum can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its availability and usage may vary depending on the region and local regulations.
